text:
Shakespeare is traditionally credited with 10 history plays (focusing on English kings), not 15. If you're including apocryphal works, Roman plays (often classified as tragedies), or other attributions, the count may vary, but here's the standard list:

1. King John
2. Richard II
3. Henry IV, Part 1
4. Henry IV, Part 2
5. Henry V
6. Henry VI, Part 1
7. Henry VI, Part 2
8. Henry VI, Part 3
9. Richard III
10. Henry VIII (also known as All Is True)

If this doesn't match what you meant by "15," please clarify!
